qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: MongoDB aggregation pipeline permits arbitrary JavaScript execution via $function operator
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding describes a missing operator-validation flaw in the MongoDB aggregation path (AggregateRouter, MongoStorageAdapter). The advisory describes a SQL injection in the PostgreSQL storage adapter's Increment operation where a dot-notation sub-key name is interpolated into SQL string literals without escaping. These are different storage adapters (MongoDB vs PostgreSQL), different root causes (missing operator blacklist vs missing SQL string escaping), and different vulnerable operations (aggregation pipeline vs Increment field update).

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described vulnerability. The advisory describes a SQL injection in the PostgreSQL storage adapter's Increment operation where dot-notation sub-key names are interpolated into SQL string literals without escaping (CWE-89). The sole qualifying finding 'mongodb_aggregation_rce' concerns the MongoDB storage adapter and missing operator validation in the aggregation pipeline path—a different storage adapter and different root cause.
evidence: Advisory: SQL injection in PostgreSQL storage adapter Increment operation via unescaped dot-notation sub-key name interpolated into SQL string literals.

Finding 'mongodb_aggregation_rce': MongoDB storage adapter aggregation pipeline missing operator validation ($function). Different adapter (MongoDB vs PostgreSQL), different operation (aggregation vs Increment), different root cause (missing operator blacklist vs missing SQL string escaping).
